#13
2 Minute Training
General Organization
Objective: Create a clean, organized environment.
We have a lot of moving pieces in the cafeteria. It is important that each of us do
our part to keep everything clean and organized so that we can work effectively.
Some examples of things we can do to stay organized:
-
Clean your area often including wiping down your station with sanitizer,
taking dishes to scullery or dishroom, and leaving your area better than you
found it
If there is an empty cart, take it to scullery
Take empty keepers where they need to go right away. CSC keepers go by
the cage; keepers that say MTC go in between scullery and the ice machines
Move empty carts to scullery
Break down boxes before putting them in the trash carts
Don't leave stuff lying around such as empty crates, boxes, etc. Pick them
up
Consolidate and organize in lockers when possible. (Ask the supervisor)
If you don't know where something goes, just ask. Don't just leave it in an open
spot.
What else can we do to be organized?

#14
2 Minute Training
Night Custodial Chemicals
Objective: Ensure employees understand the different uses for the chemicals used
during Night Custodial and be able to differentiate between them.
Pantastic
• Pantastic is washing detergent comparable to Dawn dish soap. It can be identified by its
Blue hue.
• Pantastic is the basic chemical for jobs that require scrubbing (i.e. Sinks, Drains, Pulpers
etc.).
Oasis 135: Power Force
• Powerforce is a premium cleaner/degreaser. It can be identified by its Red hue and mild
citrus aroma.
Powerforce is used to clean the floors as it is designed to help cut through heavy soil and
grease.
Oasis 146: Multi-Quat Sanitizer
.
Sanitizer is a sanitizer. It can be identified by its Red hue and disinfectant aroma.
Sanitizer is used to sanitize food contact surfaces and high contact points (i.e. Tables,
Light Switches, Locker Handles, etc.).
Consume Drop-in-a-Drain
Consume is classified as a drain cleaner. It can be identified by its Green hue and
pleasant aroma.
Consume is primarily used as an additive to the pulpers once clean.
**Use Protective Equipment**
Grease Express Fast Foam Degreaser
Degreaser is just that; a degreaser. It can be identified by its opaque Red hue and
Alcohol-like aroma.
Degreaser is used on stubborn jobs that need extra help (i.e. Flat Tops, Ovens).
EnvirOx H2Orange2 Concentrate 117
H2Orange is an Oxidizing Disinfectant/Virucide for hard non-food surfaces. It can be
identified by its lack of hue and Citrus aroma.
H2Orange is used on the Blue Belt to kill bacteria and viruses.
Lime-A-Way
Lime-A-Way is classified as a delimer. It can be identified by its lack of hue and odorless
nature.
Lime-A-Way is put in the dish machine to remove lime build up due to hard water.

#17
2 Minute Training
Pulpers
Objective: Ensure employees know how to maintain and properly use the pulper.
Visual Aid: Take the crew to dishroom or scullery to show them the pulper
The pulpers turn waste into pulp, which is then used to fertilize BYU Campus. Do not reach
into the pulper unless you have turned off the Disconnect Switch.
These items CANNOT go in the pulper:
Banana Peels
Watermelon Rinds
Pineapple Tops
Excessive Rice and
Quinoa
Eggshells
Bones
Grease/Oils
Wooden Sticks
Styrofoam
Metal
Plastic
Paper dishes
Wrappers
If the pulper isn't working:
1. Make sure the pulper is filled and turned on, with the lid closed
2. Check whether the Emergency Stop is engaged
3. If not running, drain, refill, insert cardboard and run again
4. If the "Overflow" light is flashing, spray out the back chute of the Mother pulper. Ensure
that the sensors of the Mother pulper are clear and that the laser is properly aligned
Reminders:
•
Each shift a cardboard box should be broken down and run through
the dishroom and scullery pulpers
Sunday Dinner Dishroom cleans the pulpers at the end of the day

2 Minute Training
#318
Important Cleaning: Dishroom Part 1
Objective: Ensure employees know where and how to clean problematic areas of
Dishroom.
Teaching Ideas: PLEASE SHOW THEM WHAT WE MEAN BY UNDERWORLD, NO MAN'S
LAND, AND ALL THE TRAP LOCATIONS.
*This training is highlighting hard to remember spots, not ALL cleaning*
Pit Lead and No Man's Land
• Spray out UNDERWORLD!!!
Squeegee the counter in No Man's Land. Remove all food, trash, dishes and water
• Pay particular attention to the area underneath the cup conveyer belt: clean between
the pipes and remove any debris (Time permitting, clean the walls with pantastic)
Pit Crew and Traps
·
.
Remove the 3 traps in the Pit area at the end of the shift, including the trap under the
extra blue belt (near No Man's Land). MAKE SURE YOU GET ALL OF THEM!!!
Do NOT spray food down the drains when cleaning the trap area out. Remove all food
into a trash can first
Silverware
•
Before beginning to clean the silverware machine, PLEASE go make sure the nozzles and
cone strainer have been washed! Go ask the Beverage and Salad Bar workers on line if
these two things are done.

Important Cleaning: Dish Machine
Objective: Ensure employees know where and how to clean problematic areas of
Dishroom.
Teaching Ideas: Point out specific areas
*This training is highlighting hard to remember spots, not ALL cleaning*
Cleaning the Loading Side
• A thorough spraying will save you SOOO MUCH TIME before using steel wool,

Flat and easy to reach surfaces only need a quick pass over.

Important Cleaning: Scullery
Objective: Ensure employees understand important cleaning areas in Scullery.
Teaching Ideas: Field trip to scullery to highlight cleaning areas
*This is not a training on ALL cleaning procedures in Scullery*
ANYONE can scrub and squeegee an open floor.
ONLY WINNERS scrub and squeegee thoroughly under things. BE A WINNER. If it is hard

Machines

.

•Food gets stuck under the walk in machine. SPRAY UNDER FROM THE BACK TOWARDS

#13
2 Minute Training
Allergens
Objective: For all employees to understand and implement proper allergen
procedures.
Some food allergies can be life threatening. We need to be very cautious of
how we handle food in the cafeteria, so we can keep our guests safe.
Things to be aware of:
NEVER go into the specialty meals room unless you are a
specialty meals worker!!!
Be sure to change your gloves and wash your hands often when handling
foods that could be potential allergens such as dairy, nuts, fish etc.
Avoid cross-contamination. An example of cross-contamination could be
using the same scoop for different foods.
Treat all guests with respect and be attentive to their needs. Ask your
supervisor if you have any questions.
Make sure to put out appropriate allergen signs
Condiments: Found in the box under the fruit bar.
Desserts: Found in the box by the dessert station.
Breakfast bar: Found in the box on the island.
Ice Cream: Found in the box on the island.
• Salad Bar Allergens signs: Found in the box on the island.
We also have "WARNING" signs specifically for those food items that contain
nuts. They have a red triangle with a black exclamation point in them to
signify a warning to the missionaries. These signs can be found by the blue
box by the dessert station, please put them out as needed.
A
A
*Always put all signs away at the end of a shift unless you know that the
next shift will need that sign there still.*

Tips and Tricks
Objective: To inform your crew of some tips and tricks that they might be
missing out on that could potentially make their life easier.
Examples of "Tips and Tricks":
• Beverages: At dinner, get a scullery cart and take everything that
needs to be cleaned at the same time
• Thoroughness: If you do your job thoroughly, you don't have to do it
again :D
• Scullery: Go around the cafeteria and gather dishes before you run
out so that you're always busy

suggestions. You can use them if

come ready with a few "Tips and

to share with the crew.

2 Minute Training
#18
Throwing Food Away
Objective: Ensure employees know when and what foods to throw away
Donuts (Glazed & Cake): Toss after lunch the day of delivery.
o Exception: Saturday cake donuts can be held for Sunday breakfast
then tossed.
Bagels & Muffins: Toss after Breakfast the day of delivery
o Exception: Saturday bagels and muffins can be held for Sunday
breakfast then tossed.
• Cookies: Toss after dinner 3-days after delivery.
Paninis and Sandwiches:
o Unwrapped: Toss after dinner the day of delivery.
o Wrapped: Toss after labeled best by date.
• Pre-made Salads: Toss after labeled best by date.
Milk (Bottled): Toss after dinner 5 days after best by date.
Desserts: Toss after dinner 3-days after delivery, inspect daily, consult
manager if needed.
• Whole Fruit: Check daily; toss if it's bad and consult a manager if unsure.
• Cut Fruit: Toss 7 days after date; Inspect daily, if it's bad before, consult a
manager.
Lead Trainers: a way to make this more interactive is to have someone try and
correctly name when everything needs to be thrown away - if they get it all
correct let them choose a snack from the drawer OR consult with the supervisor
and let them choose their job!

like to be informed about running low

when there are 2 pans of potatoes left)

#21
2 Minute Training
Vacuuming
Objective: For your crew to know how to properly use and maintain the
vacuums.
Things you should
not vacuum up:
• Napkins
Large chunks
of food
Utensils
• Pens/Pencils
Things you should
not do with the vacuum:
Do not take the metal prongs at the bottom of the vacuums off
•
Do not rest them on the battery
Things you should always make sure happen:
• Plug in the batteries after each use
• Hang them nicely and neatly in the vacuum closet
• Replace the filter bag whenever it is full (This should be checked after
each use to make sure that it doesn't get missed)

2 Minute Training
#22
Custodial Closet Secondary Jobs
Objective: For your crew to know how to properly do the custodial
secondary jobs.
Rag Buckets:
• Take the bucket of dirty rags from cafe west, the island, and the salads
area and bring back some clean ones.
Metal Hangers:
• The empty metal hangers from the white MTC shirts should be
removed and moved to the metal hanger stand (Located next to the
dirty clothes bin).
If the stand gets full, it should be dumped into the recycling bin
behind scullery.
Laundry:
•Any laundry that is left in the washing machine from the previous shift
running a load should be taken out and hung on the clothes lines.
Any dry clothes already on the clothes lines should be put away.
•Any dirty bathroom towels, dry mops, or dish room/cutting gloves
should be started as a load (These can also be put into the load at the
end of the shift with the table wiping towels).
Clean Uniform Pieces:
• Take all clean uniform pieces found in the custodial closet to the
basement and put away in their proper place in an organized manner.
Time to get creative!
Teach this information in a memorable way! Don't simply recite
the information!

2 Minute Training
#23
Cashier Reminders
Objective: To remind your crew of many of the small aspects of cashiering
that often get forgotten.
Main Cashier:
• P-day Clothes: Never let missionaries who are in P-day clothes into
the main cafeteria. Always send them to the sack room or to go
change
• Receipts: Always ask employees and senior missionaries if they would
like a receipt
• Closing Duties: The closing duties of a cashier are to refill the napkins,
put out more salt/pepper shakers, refill empty hand sanitizers, take all
lost/found items to the front desk, write the next menu onto the
board (including the hot/fresh bar item), AND spot sweep all tile floor
• Erasing the boards: Always to be done with a napkin and hand
sanitizer, NEVER a rag as they tend to smear the marker on the board
Sack Cashier:
• Secondary Jobs: Do the job assigned for that shift!
• Utensils/Condiments: Make sure the black pans (cambros) are filled!
Time to get creative!
Lead Trainers: This would be a great training to get creative
with how you present it! A quiz would be good, but any
other way that you think of that is memorable is also great!

#24
2 Minute Training
Trashes
Objective: To create a uniform understanding of what is expected for the
"trashes" secondary
General Information:
• Liners: extra liners are found in the red buckets hanging on the trash
carts or in the basement on the right wall.
• Tying: on both large and small cans please make sure to tie the liner
on to ensure they don't slip off.
• Large Cans: when replacing the bags on the large circular cans, make
sure to add 2 liners. These cans are often over-filled and if only one
bag is used it will rip and create a huge mess.
Carts: The carts of both cardboard and trash bags need to be taken
back and put into the compactors. If you are doing cardboard please
try to break down the boxes to create more space.
Breakfast/Lunch Specific:
•For the most part bags don't need to be
replaced, just pushed down. However, if
a can is more than 2 way full (after being
pushed down) it needs to be replaced.
Dinner Specific:
•All trash cans with food products of any
kind need to be replaced. If it is just a
small amount of food and there is
nothing else in the can, it still needs to
be replaced. This helps to prevent
insects or rodents from coming into the kitchen.

2 Minute Training
#10
Staying Productive
Objective: To teach the crews to be aware of how productive they are being
and to see things what they can do when they have a free second.
We always want to be looking for things that we can be doing. ALWAYS
PRIORITIZE GETTING SOMETHING DONE BEFORE TALKING! It is okay to talk
while being busy(:
Some ideas for staying productive:
• Ask your Supervisor: Your supervisor knows what needs to be done.
• See a need and fill it: If you notice a need, take the initiative to fill it.
• Organize your area: Make sure to leave your area better than you
found it.
• Help someone else who you have noticed is behind
• Work on secondaries or cleaning assignments
Time to get creative!
Lead Trainers: Come to the meeting with some ideas
of your own! Also, ask the crew of some of their
favorite things to do when it's not busy! Be
memorable!

2 Minute Training
Harassment & Discrimination
Objective: Inform crews of the BYU policies on harassment & non-disrcimination
Read the following policies aloud to the crew. Get verbal agreement
from your crew to treat each other with respect.
Sexual Harassment Policy
All forms of sexual harassment, including sexual assault, dating violence, domestic
violence, and stalking are contrary to the teachings of The Church of Jesus Christ of
Latter-day Saints and the Church Educational System Honor Code. Brigham Young
University prohibits sexual harassment by its personnel and students and in all its
education programs or activities.
Non-Discrimination Policy
Brigham Young University is committed to providing academic and employment
environments that are free from unlawful discrimination. Unlawful discrimination on the
basis of race, color, sex, national origin, religion, age, veteran status, genetic information
and/or disability will not be tolerated. Harassing behavior based on a protected class that
becomes so severe or pervasive that it creates a hostile environment is also unlawful.
Consent and Boundaries
Harassment and discrimination can occur in many forms in a variety of situations.
In order to prevent these issues in the MTC Cafeteria, we encourage workers to set clear
boundaries with coworkers. Say "No" when you need to! Don't let others cross the line
into what makes you uncomfortable, and do not cross boundaries that they have set with
you.
Always do your part to create a safe, comfortable work environment!
What should you do if you notice harassment/discrimination happening
around you at work?
Report to:
• Supervisor, Student Manager, Full-Time Manager
BYU Title IX Office: 1085 WSC, 801-422-8692

2 Minute Training
Medical Emergencies I
Objective: Make sure workers know how to appropriately respond to a
medical emergency at work
General Guidelines
In case of an emergency, call 911, and then call MTC Security
(801-422-9000.)
Notify the supervisor as soon as possible.
If a worker is injured in any way, even if its minor, have the supervisor
record it so the MTC can pay for any medical expenses if needed
Bleeding
Put on disposable gloves before helping someone who is bleeding
Apply pressure to stop the bleeding
If it's a minor cut, clean the wound, apply antibiotic and put on a band aid
If there is an embedded object, DON'T PULL IT OUT, apply pressure with a rolled
cloth around it to stop the bleeding
If there is severe bleeding, call 911
Make sure to wash your hands thoroughly after helping the injured person
Sprain/Fracture
Keep the body still and stabilize with splint if needed.
If it's a severe fracture, DON'T MOVE/SET BONES and wait until medical help
shows up
• Apply ice packs to prevent swelling
Burns
First Degree (red skin): remove clothing/jewelry from the area, run cool (not cold)
water, apply burn gel, and lightly bandage the burn
Second degree (blisters): same as first degree burn and seek medical attention.
(Don't break any blisters.)
Third degree (lack of pain due to nerve damage): call 911 and BYU security. Safely
remove the person from the source of the burn. (If it is an electrical burn, make
sure the power is off before approaching the person.) Make sure the person is
breathing and check for shock. DON'T APPLY WATER, lay down the person, very
loosely cover the wound with a clean cloth, and raise the burned area

#3
2 Minute Training
Medical Emergencies II
Objective: Make sure workers know how to appropriately respond to a
medical emergency at work
General Guidelines
In case of an emergency, call 911, and then call MTC Security
(801-422-9000.)
Notify the supervisor as soon as possible.
If a worker is injured in any way, even if its minor, have the supervisor
record it so the MTC can pay for any medical expenses if needed
Heat Exhaustion (When your body loses too much water or salt- usually as a result
of excessive sweating or dehydration.)
• Symptoms: Heavy Sweating, Faintness, Dizziness, Fatigue, weak and rapid pulse,
·
Move the person to a cool or air-conditioned place such as one of the offices or in
between scullery and the supervisor desk
• Lay the person down and elevate the legs and feet slightly
Have the person sip chilled water or powerade and remove tight clothing
Shock (A sudden drop in blood flow in the body caused by trauma, heatstroke, blood
loss, or an allergic reaction.)
Symptoms: extremely sweaty and clammy skin, confusion, dizziness, blueish
lips/fingernails, rapid but weak pulse, rapid breathing, and nausea or vomiting
Call emergency services and check the person's breathing and circulation, CPR
might be needed
Lay the person down, keep them still, elevate legs and feet, loosen tight clothing
and cover with a blanket or jacket, and don't let the person eat or drink anything
Heart Attack (When blood flow to the heart is severely reduced or blocked.)
Symptoms: Chest pain, pressure or tightness. Pain that spreads to the upper half
of the body. Nausea, indigestion, or abdominal pain. Shortness of breath.
Lightheadedness, dizziness, or fainting
Call the emergency numbers immediately. MTC Security has trained personnel
Clear the area around so MTC security can promptly apply CPR if needed

#4
2 Minute Training
Medical Emergencies III
Objective: Make sure workers know how to appropriately respond to a medical
emergency at work
General Guidelines
In case of an emergency, call 911, and then call MTC Security
(801-422-9000.)
Notify the supervisor as soon as possible.
If a worker is injured in any way, even if its minor, have the supervisor
record it so the MTC can pay for any medical expenses if needed
Stroke (Blocked blood flow to the brain.)
Sudden interruption of blood supply to the brain. Use the F.A.S.T. test to determine if a
person is having a stroke:
Face drooping: ask them to smile, half their face will droop
Arm weakness: ask them to raise their arms, one of them will drop
Slurred speech: ask them to say a phrase, they will say it wrong
Time to call 911: with any of the previous symptoms (even if they go away.) Time makes
the difference between someone recovering or ending up permanently disabled
Seizure (Uncontrolled electrical signals between brain cells which causes abnormal body
movements.)
This can be a scary experience but STAY CALM and help others to stay calm
Gently help the person to the ground if possible, but DO NOT hold them down or
stop their movements
Set the person on their side to help them breathe
Place a jacket or other padding under the person's head. Clear the area around
the person and remove glasses, if applicable
TIME the seizure: how long the shaking lasts, how long until they gain
consciousness. Call 911 if the seizure lasts more than 5 minutes or if the person
has multiple seizures. Also call 911 if the person is injured or has a health
condition like diabetes or heart disease.

goes off
Teaching Ideas: Know where the 4 different fire response units are (fire
extinguishers and stations) and show crew where they are at. Give a

WHAT DO YOU DO IF YOU SEE A FIRE?
You are NOT required to fight fires. If the fire seems volatile or is bigger than a
small trash can, evacuate the building. USE YOUR BEST JUDGMENT!!
Fire pull stations (typical red rectangular fire alarms): engage fire alarm
Fire suppression stations (circular fire alarms, usually silver): pull if fire is under
the hoods and the regular fire suppression system is not working. (Remember
that this is a very expensive decision. Pull if necessary, but make sure that it is an
emergency. If a supervisor or manager is nearby, let them make this decision.)
Silver Fire extinguisher: use to extinguish grease fires
Red Fire extinguisher: use to extinguish all other fires
WHAT DO YOU DO IF THE FIRE ALARM GOES OFF?
• When you hear the fire alarm, exit through the nearest door
•
• There are exits in scullery, sack room, dining area, and bakery
Meet near the trees in the Northeast parking lot by the buses
REPORT TO YOUR SUPERVISOR!
DO NOT leave the premises! Supervisors need to take accountability to
ensure you are safe
Wait until you are given the greenlight from your supervisor to go back into the
building or to go home

2 Minute Training
#6
Chemical Safety
Objective: Teach workers how to properly handle chemicals and how to react in
case of an accident with them
Teaching Ideas: Bring a chemical and the SDS/HAZCOM binder and teach the
crew how to find it/read it.
Red Chemicals
·
·
Oasis 146 Multi-Quat Sanitizer: sanitizer, food safe
Oasis 135 Power Force: floor cleaner, NOT food safe
Grease Express Fast Foam Degreaser: degreaser, DEFINITELY not food safe
General Safety
Don't mix or ingest chemicals
. Store chemicals away from food - Don't set red buckets on counters or tables
Always label chemical containers, do not use any chemical that isn't labeled
Exposure Treatment
Eyes: Rinse eyes for 15-20 minutes with an eye wash bottle (use all the content and throw
away the bottle after done). Spray affected eye away from the other eye to avoid
spreading chemicals to unaffected eye. Remove contacts if possible. If irritation persists,
get medical attention
Skin Contact: Thoroughly wash skin. For more corrosive chemicals (like degreaser),
remove any clothing with chemicals on it and wash skin for 15 minutes with cool water
and mild soap if available. Wash clothes and shoes thoroughly before reuse. Get medical
attention immediately
Ingestion: DO NOT INDUCE VOMITING. Rinse out mouth. Get medical attention. Call
Poison Control
Inhalation: Remove victim to fresh air. Call Poison Control; if symptoms occur, get medical
attention immediately
ALWAYS READ THE LABEL OF THE CHEMICAL YOU INTEND TO USE!
*CALL 911 IMMEDIATELY IF SEVERE SYMPTOMS OCCUR*
POISON CONTROL: 1-800-222-1222

Objective: Make sure workers know how to appropriately wash their hands
Teaching Ideas: Use the supervisor or another crew member for a

Why is hand washing important?
We have a lot of bacteria on our hands, especially from bathrooms and
highly touched areas around the university
•
Helps keep us from cross-contact between foods
• Helps keep food safe for customer
When to wash your hands:
At the beginning of the shift
After using the restroom
After taking a break, eating, or getting a drink
Each time you change your gloves
After touching your clothes, face or hair
When changing tasks
OFTEN
5 Steps of Handwashing
1. Get hands wet with warm, running water.
2. Use soap, scrubbing hands, in-between fingers, and under fingernails.
3. Scrub for at least 20 seconds. (Remind workers this is about the time it takes to
sing "happy birthday."
4. Rinse thoroughly.
5. Use paper towels to dry.
REMEMBER! Do not touch your face with your gloves. If you do, you need
to get new ones

Drain
Remove and clean the drain cover with steel wool and Pantastic. Clean beneath the drain
pipe's funnel-like opening. Clean underneath the panel on the right wall. you should be able to

Plastic Curtains
Remove the plastic curtains. Use steel wool and Pantastic to scrub between the plastic sheets.

Operating the Pulper:
Note: There are three locations for Pulper functioning:
1) The pulper press (aka the "Mother Pulper") located through the cafeteria in a room north of
the Wall of Salad
2) The Sullery Pulper: located in the Sculler
3) The Dishroom Pulper: located in (you guessed it) the dish-room
Filling the Pulpers
First make sure all drain levers are closed
Like Aspens and members of the Church, the pulpers are independent and yet connected. In the
morning the three big, round food pulpers need to be filled with water. The pulper in the dish-room
needs to be filled high enough so the trough has about 3 inches of water. Failure to do so will drain the
mother pulper of water and the system won't work.
The system does not need to be drained after each shift. The pulper system should be drained and the
pulpers cleaned each night.
If there is a problem with odor then we may need to clean and drain the pulper after each meal, but for
now just fill the pulpers in the morning and drain them at night.
A box worth of cardboard should be fed through the pulpers (excluding the mother pulper)during each
shift in order to keep the pulper blades sharp.
Turning the Pulpers on
There are levers and/or buttons related to turning the pulpers on and off
If the pulpers won't turn on after turning all the switches to the on position and pressing the buttons,
typically there is a problem with the safety magnets. Check all panels and covers associated with the
pulpers to make sure all magnets are connected. If the pulper still fails to work properly then talk with a
manager or our maintenance man Rob.

SETTING UP DISHROOM & SCULLERY
A GUIDE FOR SUPERVISORS AND LEAD TRAINERS
Dishroom:
1. Turn on both dishroom fans
2. Close the drains in the cup machine by pushing the small internal levers down all the way,
close the doors, and turn it on. It should start autofilling.
3. Check the baskets in the dish machine for food, check to ensure all parts of the dish
machine are placed inside properly
a. Close dish machine doors and drains
b. Turn on the dish machine
c. Repeat with the other machine as needed
4. Check the silverware machine, if the water is not clear, drain the machine and fill it again.
a. Ensure that the clean-side of the countertop is actually clean. If not, clean it with
pantastic.
5. Check the mobile sink, if it is not adequately clean, rinse it out before use.
a. You do not have to fill the mobile sink, but if you have extra time, it would be very
helpful
6. Fill the pulper by pressing the green button. You should hear water start to fill the pulper.
7. Check to ensure all 7 traps are put in place
Scullery:
1. Turn on ventilation (on the wall just inside of the dock door as you enter scullery) and both
fans (one in the bucket shelves and the other on the hand sink-side of the
pan machine)
2. (optional) Close the walk-in machine drain by either lifting the grate and turning the
handle clockwise, or using the tool on the wall to turn the handle. Only do this if you plan
to use the walk-in machine.
a. Turn the black dial to the "FILL" position (left)
3. Close the pan machine drain by turning the handle clockwise (found inside of the hole at
the bottom right of the machine)
a. Turn the pan machine on
4. Fill the pulper by pushing the green button. You should hear water start to fill the pulper.
5. Begin filling the scullery sinks. The wash sink should be full of hot water (but not too hot
to where you will burn yourself trying to take dishes out), the rinse sink should be full of
the same, and the sanitizing sink should be cool water. Both the soap in the wash sink and
the sanitizer in the sanitizing sink should run for about 20-30 seconds.

apply at this entrance point as other campus entrances.

not speeding.

parking lot will not be available on Saturdays or when a funeral
is scheduled. Emails will be sent to make you aware of
funerals.

push the button to exit.

person to enter or leave.

enter the main MTC vehicle entrance on Wednesdays between
the hours of 12:30 to approximately 2:30 pm.
We acknowledge that there might be open parking stalls available,
other than in the garage, during this time period. However, there are
many moving parts at play and parking limitations during that time.

Ovens
Tools: Red Bucket with Pantastic and water, steel wool, Degreaser, hose, paper towels, rubber
apron from Scullery, arm-length "cow" gloves, green rubber gloves, face mask, goggles, masking
tape.
THIS CANNOT BE UNDERESTIMATED. CLEANING OVENS WITHOUT PROPER
PROTECTION WILL HURT YOU. YOU HAVE BEEN WARNED.
Prep:
ALL PPE is required when cleaning ovens. A rubber
apron from Scullery, green rubber gloves, arm-length
"cow" gloves taped around arms, a face mask, goggles,
and as always, your full Night Custodial uniform (white
shirt, blue ball cap, long pants, and rubber boots).
Procedure:
Make sure all ovens are off.
Remove oven rack and load onto a cart. Take racks to
Scullery. Load into the pan machine and run for a
"Long" cycle.
Spray down the interior of the ovens with Degreaser
getting all areas. Shut the doors and let it sit for ten
minutes.
• Using Pantastic diluted in water and steel wool scrub all
areas of the ovens. Yes, this includes the doors, the ceiling, the walls, and floors. Pay
close attention to the back grill.
WHILE CLEANING, TAKE CARE OF YOURSELF. TAKE BREAKS AS
NECESSARY. DRINK WATER, GET FRESH AIR. THE DEGREASER IS A
STRONG CHEMICAL.

Rinse the ovens with the hose. Make sure to get ALL the chemicals out of there.
• Using paper towels, wipe down the interior of the ovens to remove the remaining
grease/chemicals.
• Replace the oven racks.
Clean the pan machine.

Pulpers
Tools: Bucket of Pantastic, green scrubber, steel wool (if green scrubber doesn't work), 2 bottles
of Consume, and green gloves
Dishroom Pulper:
1. The pulper should still be running. Turn it off by pressing the yellow button. If that
button doesn't work, press the red button. This should stop the pulper.
2. Turn the emergency switch on the wall panel to the "OFF" position.
3. Open the drain to the pulper. Pull the yellow lever underneath so it is vertical.
4. While the pulper drains, clean the trough. First, spray the whole trough pushing as
much gunk as you can into the pulper. Then quickly use the green scrubber and the
Pantastic to clean spots where the spraying wouldn't remove the gunk. Rinse.
5. Clean the pulper using the green scrubber and Pantastic.
a. Scrub the top of the pulper free of any visible pulper juices. Rinse it.
b. Scrub the top and bottom of the pulper lid free of visible juices. Rinse it and
set it aside. Put the edge of the green scrubber along/inside edged corners of
the metal ring just inside the top of pulper to scrub them clean.
c. Thoroughly spray the pulper to get rid of most of the gunk. Don't forget to
spray under the rim pointing up as well as at the inner walls of the pulper.
Spray the magnetic mechanism (the metal rod pointing into the middle of the
pulper) from below. This will drop food to the bottom of the pulper to be
picked up more easily.
d. Pull any glass out of the pulper, and put it into the glass bucket (along the east
wall of Dishroom). Use your green gloves to prevent cutting your hands.
Pull out and throw away any other trash in the pulper.
e. Scrub everything with Pantastic and rinse everything. Be sure to clean the
following areas that are often missed:
i. The top rim, especially where it connects to the walls of the pulper
ii. The ceiling and inner walls of the pulper
iii. The area where the trough meets the pulper
iv. The magnetic mechanism, especially where it connects to the wall
6. Close the pulper drain by turning the yellow lever to the original position. Refill the
pulper with water so that it covers the three fins. Dump in half a bottle of Consume.
7. Clean the seven food traps in the Dishroom as well as all the pull-out pieces.
a. The two nearest pit underneath the blue belt
b. Two, one on each belt leading to each dish machine.
c. One underneath the cups rack conveyor (by 2nd dish machine)
d. One underneath "No Man's Land"; underneath the start of the rack conveyor
e. One outside on dish return behind the steel door.
Scullery Pulper:
1. Follow numbers one and two under "Dishroom pulper" to shut off the pulper.
2. Open the drain just as before.
3. While it drains, use the Pantastic to scrub the table/counter and wall. Rinse.
a. Also spray the crack from which pulper water flows.
4. Clean the pulper using Pantastic like the dishroom pulper.

Updated 5/24/19
a. Scrub and rinse the blue curtains.
b. Clean the front and back of the frame where the blue curtain hangs. Rinse.
c. Scrub under the ledge. Rinse.
d. Scrub anything else that may have pulper juice on it.
5. Close the pulper drain.
6. Refill the pulper with water. Use the two sprayers that hang down. (They can refill it
while you go drain the mother pulper.) Fill it so that those three fins are under water.
7. When the pulper is full, dump half of a bottle of Consume into the pulper. Leave full.
8. Clean the 3 drains in Scullery.
Mother Pulper:
1. Get the keys from the assistant manager's office to unlock the mother pulper room.
Put the keys back immediately; others may need to use them.
2. Drain the mother pulper. The valve is on the backside of the pulper. Point it directly
away from the pulper. (You should have time to open the valve and possibly the next
two steps before you need to return to scullery to stop filling the pulper.)
3. Grab the lid and remove it. Set it aside.
4. From a distance, spray all the sludge that you can see using the hose. Get close to it to
spray all the stuff that you couldn't see from a distance.
5. Scrub the mother pulper using Pantastic and the blue, curved scrubber that is found
hanging next to the pulper.
a. Scrub the inside of the lid that you removed.
b. Scrub the inner walls.
c. Scrub all the way around the cylindrical pipe thing.
d. Scrub underneath the cylindrical pipe thing.
e. Scrub anything else that has pulper juice on it.
f. Scrub what you can from the pipe at the back of the mother pulper.
g. Rinse everything.
6. Close the drain and refill the pulper so that the water level is an inch or two below the
pipe at the back of the mother pulper.
a. Use the second hose that hangs into the pulper.
7. While it fills, clean the floors around the mother pulper.
a. Spray Powerforce and some water on the floor.
b. Scrub the floor with a floor brush.
c. Rinse.
d. Squeegee the water into the drain under the mother pulper. The squeegee may
not fit behind the pulper. If so, don't try to squeegee that.
8. Clean the drain under the pulper using Pantastic. Scrub it and the grate. Rinse.
9. Once the pulper is full, add half of a bottle of Consume.
Dish Return:
Dump one half of the remaining Consume (this will be a fourth of a bottle) into the
back trough on the opposite side as the drain. Leave it there. Don't rinse it.
2. Spot clean this trough with your green scrubber.
3. Repeat steps 1 and 2 on the other dish return trough.

Main Dish Hoods
Tools: Red Bucket with Pantastic and water, steel wool, metal butter knife (for scraping), ladders
(For over flattops/grill), hose, squeegee, paper towel, patience.
Procedure:
• Fill the pan machine in Scullery, take the large wireframe cart back to Main Dish with
you.
• Remove all cooking equipment/utensils/tools from under the Hoods you will be working
on.
• Remove hood panels by twisting the spring-loaded hammers and remove the hanging
safety hook.
Remove drip trays.
• Using the cart, take all pieces to Scullery and load them into the pan machine. Run using
the "Long" cycle which takes roughly eight minutes.
●
Return thence to Main Dish, and taking up your steel wool and scraper bestowed from
the heavens above, begin your quest to vanquish the darkness brought about by the
hordes of grease upon the Cafeteria. Yea, with power and might rivaling the strength of
Hercules, scrub the heck out of these wretched contraptions. As they are baptized in holy
Pantastic, it shall rain upon you like a torrential downpour. And when you feel the water
seeping into the fiber of your very being, you shall know it is finished.
Once during your labors scrubbing, return to Scullery and run the pieces through another
"Long" Cycle,
Once it is good, rinse the area well with the hose.
Using squeegees and paper towels, dry everything you can reach.
Once everything is dry, bring all the pieces from Scullery (drain the pan machine) and
place them back in their proper slots.
Replace all equipment/utensils/tools that were previously removed to their proper homes.
Sanitize Steam Pot lids as necessary.
• Go back to Scullery and clean the pan machine.
